Data Science in the Age of Agentic AI

Data professionals might feel a bit disoriented these days. New agentic tools keep arriving and generating buzz. Concerns around AI replacing human-filled roles keep growing — including in areas deemed safe not so long ago.

 

What does the future hold for data scientists, engineers, and analysts? How can they adapt to a rapidly changing landscape for hiring and career growth? What does excelling mean for a data practitioner when an AI agent can likely accomplish in seconds tasks that used to take days?

 

This week's highlights offer sharp insights, actionable advice, and a healthy (if nuanced) sense of optimism.

Beyond Code Generation: AI for the Full Data Science Workflow. 

 Instead of worrying about being displaced by AI, Yu Dong suggests data scientists should find smart and effective ways to harness it across the entire data pipeline. "The bigger shift," Yu argues, "is that AI can now participate in a much more end-to-end data science workflow."

Read More

From Dashboards to Decisions: Rethinking Data & Analytics in the Age of AI

Continuing along similar lines, Rashi Desai explores the many ways AI agents, data foundations, and human-centered analytics are coming together to produce more robust decision-making processes. 

Read More

The Complete Guide to AI Implementation for Chief Data & AI Officers in 2026

If you lead a data organization — large or small — don't miss Hugo Lu's latest deep dive, in which he introduces a comprehensive framework for effective, growth-oriented prioritization of AI initiatives .

Read More

From Batch to Real-Time: What It Actually Takes to Modernize Your Data Pipelines

In this session, Kim Fessel joins Jess Ramos of Big Data Energy and Manish Patel, GM of Data Integration at CData, to talk through what pipeline modernization actually looks like in practice. Join us live to learn when CDC is the right move versus when it’s overkill, and what separates teams that modernize incrementally from those that get stuck in planning mode.

Register Today

This Week's Most-Read Stories

In case you missed them, here are three articles that resonated with a wide audience in the past week:

  • Building a Personal AI Agent in a Couple of Hours, by Ivo Bernardo
    We are living in a Brave New World and prototyping just got fun.

  • How to Make Claude Code Better at One-Shotting Implementations, by Eivind Kjosbakken
    Make your coding agent more efficient.

     

  • How Can A Model 10,000× Smaller Outsmart ChatGPT?, by Moulik Gupta
    Why thinking longer can matter more than being bigger.

Other Recommended Reads

You might be interested in some of the other topics we covered recently — so we hope you explore these excellent reads:

  • I Built a Podcast Clipping App in One Weekend Using Vibe Coding, by Katy Hagerty
    Rapid prototyping with Replit, AI agents, and minimal manual coding.

  • Prompt Caching with the OpenAI API: A Full Hands-On Python Tutorial, by Maria Mouschoutzi
    A step-by-step guide to making your OpenAI apps faster, cheaper, and more efficient.

  • Using OpenClaw as a Force Multiplier: What One Person Can Ship with Autonomous Agents, by Nick Lawson
    It's easier than ever to 10x your output with agentic AI.

  • Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Deep Dive, by Clara Chong
    How to optimize context, a precious finite resource for AI agents.

  • Building Human-In-The-Loop Agentic Workflows, by Kenneth Leung
    Understanding how to set up human-in-the-loop (HITL) agentic workflows in LangGraph.

From our sister site, roadmap.sh

AI Agents Roadmap: Build Systems That Act

This roadmap outlines the transition from LLM fundamentals to autonomous systems that reason and execute tasks. It focuses on building agents that can perceive, plan, act with tools, and maintain long-term memory.

Explore the Roadmap

Meet Our New Authors

We hope you take the time to explore top-notch work from our newest contributors:

  • Peter Zakrzewski published a thoughtful systems design diagnosis of hallucination, corrigibility, and the structural gap that scaling cannot close.

  • S. M. Navin Nayer Anik focused on building multi-node training pipelines in a thorough, step-by-step guide.

  • Amit Shreiber unpacked the geometric foundations you need to master in order to understand the dot product.
